2604.12470.title: Intelligent ROI-Based Vehicle Counting Framework for Automated Traffic Monitoring
2604.12470.paper_summary: Accurate vehicle counting through video surveillance is crucial for efficient traffic management. However, achieving high counting accuracy while ensuring computational efficiency remains a challenge. To address this, we propose a fully automated, video-based vehicle counting framework designed to optimize both computational efficiency and counting accuracy. Our framework operates in two distinct phases: \textit{estimation} and \textit{prediction}. In the estimation phase, the optimal region of interest (ROI) is automatically determined using a novel combination of three models based on detection scores, tracking scores, and vehicle density. This adaptive approach ensures compatibility with any detection and tracking method, enhancing the framework's versatility. In the prediction phase, vehicle counting is efficiently performed within the estimated ROI. We evaluated our framework on benchmark datasets like UA-DETRAC, GRAM, CDnet 2014, and ATON. Results demonstrate exceptional accuracy, with most videos achieving 100\% accuracy, while also enhancing computational efficiency, making processing up to four times faster than full-frame processing. The framework outperforms existing techniques, especially in complex multi-road scenarios, demonstrating robustness and superior accuracy. These advancements make it a promising solution for real-time traffic monitoring.
2604.12470.paper_summary_zh: 準確的車輛計數透過視頻監控對於高效的交通管理至關重要。<lb/>然而，在確保計算效率的同時實現高計數準確性仍然是一個挑戰。<lb/>為了解決這個問題，我們提出了一個完全自動化的基於視頻的車輛計數框架，旨在優化計算效率和計數準確性。<lb/>我們的框架分為兩個不同的階段：\textit{估計}和\textit{預測}。<lb/>在估計階段，通過基於檢測分數、跟踪分數和車輛密度的三個模型的新穎組合，自動確定最佳感興趣區域（ROI）。<lb/>這種自適應方法確保了與任何檢測和跟踪方法的兼容性，增強了框架的多功能性。<lb/>在預測階段，車輛計數在估計的ROI內高效執行。<lb/>我們在UA-DETRAC、GRAM、CDnet 2014和ATON等基準數據集上評估了我們的框架。<lb/>結果顯示出卓越的準確性，大多數視頻達到100\%的準確率，同時提高了計算效率，使處理速度比全幀處理快四倍。<lb/>該框架超越了現有技術，特別是在複雜的多道路場景中，展現了穩健性和優越的準確性。<lb/>這些進展使其成為實時交通監控的有前景的解決方案。
2604.12470.author: Mohamed A. Abdelwahab et.al.
2604.12470.authors: Mohamed A. Abdelwahab, Zaynab Al-Ariny, Mahmoud Fakhry, El-Sayed Hasaneen
